subject: Biology
title: Muscle Cells: Built to Contract
description: A fast breakdown of Muscle Cells: Built to Contract for builders.

hook.text: Muscle cells contain fibers that slide past each other, shortening the cell to produce force.

buildup.text: Skeletal muscle fibers are long and multinucleated, bundled together like cables.

discovery.text: Actin and myosin filaments grab and pull each other using energy from ATP.

twist.text: Heart muscle cells beat on their own—they generate their own electrical rhythm.

climax.text: Three types: skeletal for movement, cardiac for heartbeat, smooth for organs.

punchline.text: Cells that pull. That's all muscle is.

quiz.question: What two proteins interact to make muscles contract?
